subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics built from independent sources (IMF, World Bank, UNHCR/OCHA, V-Dem, Freedom House, RSF, HRW, Transparency International, ICG, ACLED) + international wire (Reuters/AFP/AP/Al Jazeera); Chadian state media EXCLUDED there, economics IMF-validated.

A hereditary military regime: Idriss Deby ruled 1990-2021; on his death his son Mahamat Deby took power by junta, then won a contested May 2024 election (~61%) that his own PM-rival Succes Masra rejected as rigged - a dynastic succession dressed as a transition.

Standout drags: a Sudan-war refugee influx exceeding ~1.2M in the east (one of the world's largest), the Lake Chad Basin Boko Haram/ISWAP insurgency, and among the world's worst poverty (~84% multidimensionally poor) and climate exposure; the 2025 French military withdrawal removes a long-standing security backstop and opens a Russia-courting drift.
Currency anchor: the Central African CFA franc (CEMAC), hard-pegged to the euro under a French convertibility guarantee - monetary stability without monetary sovereignty; not a reserve currency.

economic.notes: A peg-anchored, low-inflation but oil-dependent and very low-income economy on an IMF program, with deep fiscal fragility and a resource-curse profile.

fiscal_state.notes: On a 2025 IMF ECF arrangement with debt stabilizing near ~32% of GDP and a narrowing non-oil deficit, but heavily oil-revenue-dependent with substantial downside risks. (IMF 2024 Art IV; 2025 ECF/DSA)

monetary_independence.notes: No own monetary policy, but the CEMAC CFA franc's hard euro peg with a French convertibility guarantee delivers credible stability in a fragile region. (BEAC; IMF)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The CFA franc is a regional currency, not an international reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: Low and peg-anchored near the CEMAC norm, though food-price shocks from conflict and refugee strain add volatility. (IMF)

banking_stability.notes: Part of the COBAC-supervised CEMAC system - shallow, low credit penetration, but peg-backed. (BEAC; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Acute resource-curse profile: oil is ~90% of exports via a single Cameroon pipeline; landlocked, undiversified, minimal industrial base. (IMF; World Bank)

institutional.notes: A personalized dynastic regime with weak rule of law, pervasive corruption, thin state capacity, and a repressed press.

rule_of_law.notes: Personalized military rule with no judicial independence and entrenched patronage corruption (TI CPI ~21/100, among the world's worst). (Transparency International; Freedom House; V-Dem)

statistical_integrity.notes: Weak capacity but IMF-engaged on macro data; political and conflict-zone data unreliable. (IMF)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Patronage-laden and thinly resourced, with little reach into conflict and refugee-hosting peripheries. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: Restricted press with arrests of journalists - three jailed in 2025 over alleged Wagner ties. (RSF; HRW)

political_social.notes: A dynastic succession over chronic instability, a vast refugee influx, and acute climate-and-conflict stress on social cohesion.

political_stability.notes: Mahamat Deby inherited power by junta after his father's 2021 death and won a contested 2024 vote rejected as rigged by his rival - dynastic continuity, not a stable settlement. (HRW; Al Jazeera; Reuters)

civil_liberties.notes: Rated 'Not Free'; the 2022 'Black Thursday' protest crackdown killed scores, with continued detentions. (Freedom House; HRW; Amnesty)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Hosts over ~1.2M Sudanese refugees plus Boko-Haram-displaced - relatively open borders but at a breaking point with severe underfunding. (UNHCR; OCHA)

social_cohesion.notes: North-south and ethnic cleavages, herder-farmer conflict, and Lake Chad insurgency spillover strain an already fragile social fabric. (ICG; ACLED)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing - a potential dividend but currently a strain given ~84% multidimensional poverty. (World Bank; UNDP MPI)

geopolitical.notes: A former French security anchor drifting after the 2025 troop withdrawal, with an active internal insurgency and Sudan-war spillover.

alliance_reliability.notes: Ended the French military presence in 2025, removing a long-standing backstop and opening a Russia-courting drift, with ambiguous Sudan-conflict alignment (alleged RSF/UAE conduit). (ICG; CRS; Reuters)

conflict_involvement.notes: Active Lake Chad Basin Boko Haram/ISWAP insurgency plus massive Sudan-war spillover on the eastern border; not a combatant state but heavily destabilized. (ACLED; ICG; UNHCR)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Not broadly sanctioned; operates within the CEMAC regional FX framework with its convertibility guarantee. (BEAC; IMF)

physical_practical.notes: Among the world's harshest physical-risk profiles: extreme climate exposure, near-absent healthcare, minimal infrastructure, and conflict-zone insecurity.

climate.notes: ND-GAIN bottom-5 - severe Sahel drought, desertification, Lake Chad collapse, recurrent flood, least-prepared. (ND-GAIN; WB)

healthcare.notes: Among the world's weakest systems - roughly 3.7 doctors per 100,000, minimal services outside N'Djamena, strained further by refugee inflows. (WHO; World Bank)

infrastructure.notes: Landlocked with very low electrification, sparse paved roads, and dependence on a single export pipeline through Cameroon. (World Bank)

crime_safety.notes: High insecurity from insurgency, banditry, and intercommunal violence, concentrated in the Lake Chad and eastern border zones. (ACLED; OSAC)

disaster_insurance.notes: Negligible insurance penetration against high drought/flood exposure. (inference)

summary: A Sahelian, landlocked, oil-dependent state run as a hereditary military regime - Idriss Deby ruled three decades until his 2021 death, after which his son Mahamat seized power by junta and legitimized it in a contested 2024 vote his own rival rejected as rigged. The forward picture is heavily negative: an insurgency on Lake Chad, a Sudan-war refugee influx exceeding 1.2 million in the east, among the world's worst poverty and climate exposure, near-absent healthcare and infrastructure, weak rule of law, and a repressed press. The 2025 French military withdrawal removes a long-standing backstop and invites a Russia-courting drift. The only meaningful offsets are the CFA-franc euro peg's monetary stability, low inflation, and an IMF-anchored fiscal program - all real but thin against a deeply fragile base.
